Introduction:

ROCm is an open-source GPU computing platform developed by NVIDIA Corporation, which provides a set of software libraries and tools for developing applications that run on GPUs. One of the key features of ROCm is its support for multiple GPU architectures, including those from NVIDIA, AMD, and Intel. This technical note will provide instructions on how to validate ROCm on gfx900, a graphics processing unit (GPU) with 128 compute units.

Step 1: Install ROCm

Before validating ROCm on gfx900, you need to install the ROCm software development kit (SDK). The installation process is straightforward and can be done using the following steps:

  1. Download the latest version of the ROCm SDK from the official website (https://rocm.github.io/downloads
